# simulator/results/

This directory contains CSV output files generated by the comparison simulators.

---

## Generated files

| File | Generated by | Contents |
|---|---|---|
| `electronic_binary_vs_scd.csv` | `electronic_binary_vs_scd.py` | BCD vs SCD metrics under bit-flip noise |
| `photonic_binary_vs_obqc.csv` | `photonic_binary_vs_obqc.py` | Binary vs optical bead metrics under Gaussian noise |
| `qudit_inspired_channel.csv` | `qudit_inspired_channel.py` | Toy qudit channel throughput metrics |
| `optical_medium_comparison.csv` | `optical_medium_comparison.py` | SER by medium type across M and D configurations |
| `hybrid_quantum_support_energy.csv` | `hybrid_quantum_support_energy.py` | Toy energy model: OBQC aux layer vs baseline (E_cryo unchanged) |
| `pattern_vs_binary_operation_cost.csv` | `pattern_vs_binary_operation_cost.py` | Abstract cost model: pattern pipeline vs binary pipeline |

---

## How to generate

```bash
# Run all simulators at once
python run_comparisons.py

# Or run each individually
python electronic_binary_vs_scd.py
python photonic_binary_vs_obqc.py
python qudit_inspired_channel.py
```

---

## Notes

- CSV files are deterministic: the same script with the same seed always produces the same output.
- Default random seed: 42. Override with `--seed N`.
- CSV files may be large for high trial counts. Do not commit large generated files.
- Small demonstration results (default settings) are acceptable to commit for reproducibility.
- All CSV files use UTF-8 encoding with standard comma delimiters.

---

## Disclaimer

These CSV files contain results from simplified simulation models.
They are NOT experimental measurements from physical hardware.
They are NOT proofs of any computational advantage.
They are reproducible, falsifiable outputs from explicitly stated model assumptions.

See [docs/comparative-simulation-framework.md](../docs/comparative-simulation-framework.md) for full explanation.
